context: Risk-off, macro-gated tape. Wednesday's (Jul 8) FOMC minutes landed hawkish — the Fed held 3.50-3.75% but stripped its cut hints and 9 of 19 officials now pencil in a hike, with staff lifting inflation forecasts on tariff and oil pass-through; the same day the Iran ceasefire collapsed and oil jumped ~5%. Fear & Greed sits in Extreme Fear (~20) and the Coinbase premium has been negative for 50 straight sessions, so US spot demand is weak even as offshore whales accumulate. The week's binary is June CPI (~Jul 14); into it, majors are ranging and the honest bias across BTC / XRP / SUI / SOL is NEUTRAL — trade the levels, not a thesis, and keep size light ahead of the print.

tickers.thesis: XRP is the top-priority name on catalyst density, not on direction. CLARITY Act passage odds have fallen to ~42% (from ~73% earlier in the year) as the DeFi and stablecoin provisions drew fire and the 60-vote Senate path narrowed; reconciling the Senate Banking and Agriculture versions before the August recess is a genuine binary — passage re-rates toward $1.60-2.20 (Standard Chartered models $4-8B of first-year ETF inflows vs ~$1.44B so far), a fail points back to $0.80-1.00. Price is holding ~$1.10 with mild relative strength (+3.8% 7d) into a hawkish-Fed, pre-CPI tape. Bias NEUTRAL — this is a wait-for-the-trigger name: long only on a sweep-and-reclaim of the $1.07 24h low with NY-session momentum; a daily close below $1.055 or a markup-collapse headline invalidates.

setup.entry: Long the sweep-and-reclaim of $1.07 into the $1.08-1.09 OTE (70.5%), NY killzone momentum only
setup.invalidation: Daily close < $1.055; or a CLARITY markup-collapse / shelved-bill headline

catalysts: CLARITY Act — passage odds ~42% (down from ~73%); Senate Banking + Ag versions must reconcile before the Aug recess. THE binary. [Regulatory-US]
XRP spot ETFs — ~$1.44B cumulative since launch; Standard Chartered models $4-8B first-year IF CLARITY passes. [ETF & Flows]
Macro tie-in — hawkish Jul-8 Fed minutes + June CPI (~Jul 14) gate risk appetite for any re-rate. [Macro-Risk]

tickers.thesis: BTC is holding $62.7k with surprising resilience (+3.3% 7d) despite a hawkish trifecta: the Jul-8 FOMC minutes removed cut hints (9 of 19 dots now see a hike) and lifted inflation forecasts, the Iran ceasefire collapsed the same day sending oil +~5%, and June CPI (~Jul 14) looms as the week's binary. Under the surface US demand is weak — the Coinbase premium has been negative for 50 straight sessions (a record) even as whales absorbed ~270k BTC in June through OTC/offshore channels. Bias NEUTRAL into the CPI gate; the tape is a $60k-65k range. Fade rallies into $63.5k-65k supply, buy only a $60k sweep-and-reclaim; a daily close below $58.5k breaks the cycle-floor thesis.

setup.entry: Fade rallies into the $63.5k-65k OTE short zone (below weekly supply); or long only on a $60k sweep-and-reclaim
setup.stop: $65,400 (above R2 + buffer) for the short leg
setup.invalidation: 1h close > $65,000 reclaims supply; long thesis needs a $60k sweep-reclaim, dead below $58.5k daily close

catalysts: FOMC minutes (Jul 8) read hawkish — held 3.50-3.75%, 9 of 19 see a hike, cut hints removed; risk-off. [Macro-Risk]
June CPI ~Jul 14 (BLS) — the week's binary; tariff + oil pass-through raises hot-print risk. [Macro-Risk]
Iran ceasefire collapsed Jul 8, oil +~5% — fresh inflation impulse + risk-off overhang. [Macro-Risk]
Whales absorbed ~270k BTC in June via OTC while Coinbase premium stayed negative 50 days. [Whale & Smart Money]
liq_above: Short stops above the post-minutes high

tickers.thesis: SUI is today's relative leader (+2.2% 24h) as the insider-unlock cliff phase winds down and the ETF pipeline builds — the SEC approved the first 2x SUI product (TXXS via 21Shares), Grayscale filed an S-1 for a dedicated Sui Trust, and four spot applications sit with the SEC. Offsetting that: the Jul-1 unlock (13.72M SUI, ~$9.4M, part of a ~$73M weekly wave) keeps a persistent supply drip, and at $0.73 SUI is a thin, high-beta name that will track BTC's macro tape. Bias NEUTRAL; long the $0.70 sweep-reclaim toward $0.78, invalidate on a daily close below $0.66.

setup.entry: Long the $0.70 sweep-and-reclaim into the $0.71-0.72 OTE; watch-only unless a clean reclaim prints
setup.invalidation: Daily close < $0.66; or BTC losing $60k drags high-beta alts

tickers.thesis: SOL embodies the 'ETF paradox' — more than $1B of cumulative spot-ETF inflows (Bitwise BSOL, Fidelity FSOL) yet price has faded to ~$78 as venture/insider unlocks absorb the institutional bid. Fundamentals keep progressing (Firedancer is live on mainnet across 207 validators; Clearstream added SOL custody Jul 8, expanding EU institutional rails) and Standard Chartered's year-end target is $250 — but that's a risk-on story, not a hawkish-Fed pre-CPI week. Bias NEUTRAL-to-soft; the $72-85 range governs. Fade rallies into $80-85; a loss of $72 opens $66.

setup.entry: Fade rallies into the $80-85 OTE short zone; or long only on a $72 sweep-and-reclaim
setup.stop: $86.5 (above R2 + buffer) for the short leg
setup.invalidation: 1h close > $85 reclaims the shelf; long thesis needs a $72 reclaim, dead below $66

catalysts: SOL ETF paradox — cumulative inflows >$1B (BSOL, FSOL) but price faded as venture unlocks absorb demand. [ETF & Flows]
Clearstream (Deutsche Börse) added SOL custody Jul 8 — EU institutional access. [CME & Institutional]
Firedancer live on mainnet (207 validators, tens of millions of txns) — throughput/resilience upgrade. [Protocol & Upgrades]
Std Chartered YE-2026 target $250 (near-term analyst $150) vs live ~$78. [L1 Rotation]

liq_below: Longs below the mid-June range floor

insights.title: Sentiment flat: F&G 22 (Extreme Fear), Δ +2 vs yesterday
insights.body: Crypto Fear & Greed Index printed 22 (Extreme Fear) at 06:51Z — +2 vs yesterday's reading. F&G under 30 historically aligns with bounce-watch zones; under 20 with capitulation. Combined with the recent price action and live headlines (see LIVE FEED), the regime is risk-off until a sweep-and-reclaim candle prints. No new longs until F&G stops falling AND BTC reclaims its 24h high.

insights.title: Today's macro releases (auto from ForexFactory)
insights.body: USD high-impact releases registered today: ✓ ISM Services PMI → forecast 54.2 | prev 54.5; ✓ FOMC Meeting Minutes → high-impact USD event. Live event data pulled from ForexFactory weekly JSON at 06:51Z.

insights.title: Hawkish Fed + oil shock is the whole tape
insights.body: The Jul-8 minutes removing cut language while 9 of 19 dots flip to a hike is a regime signal, not a one-day event — and it landed the same day the Iran ceasefire collapsed and oil ran +5%. That is the worst combo for BTC: tighter-for-longer AND a fresh inflation impulse. Until June CPI (~Jul 14) resolves, rallies are sells-into-supply, not breakouts.

insights.title: BTC's resilience is real but hollow underneath
insights.body: BTC is +3.3% on the week and holding $62.7k despite the hawkish print — but the Coinbase premium has been negative for 50 consecutive sessions, the longest streak on record. The bid is offshore/OTC (whales took ~270k BTC in June), not US-regulated. A rally that does not turn the Coinbase premium positive is a rally to fade.

insights.title: XRP is a coin-flip, and the odds just got worse
insights.body: CLARITY passage odds fell to ~42% from ~73% as the DeFi and stablecoin provisions drew fire and the 60-vote path narrowed. With the August recess as a hard deadline, XRP's payoff is now asymmetric to the downside on headline risk. Do not pre-position — let the markup-vote signal print first.

insights.title: The ETF-inflow story is not a price story right now
insights.body: SOL has >$1B of cumulative ETF inflows and SUI just got its first leveraged product — yet both are flat-to-down because venture/insider unlocks are absorbing the bid. In a risk-off tape, 'inflows' without price confirmation is a value trap; wait for a reclaim, not a flow headline.

insights.title: Extreme Fear is a condition, not a trigger
insights.body: F&G ~20 tells you sentiment is washed out, but with a hawkish Fed and CPI ahead it is not a mean-reversion buy on its own. The reclaim is what matters: BTC needs to hold $60k on a sweep, XRP needs $1.07 reclaimed. Buying Extreme Fear blind into a binary week is how you catch a knife.

hidden_signals.body: Fear & Greed at 22 (Extreme Fear), Δ +2 vs yesterday — sub-30 zones historically align with bounce setups but require a sweep-and-reclaim candle to confirm

Coinbase Premium Index negative for 50 straight sessions — the longest streak on record. US-regulated demand is structurally weak; every BTC bounce here is offshore-led and suspect until the premium flips positive.

Whale accumulation of ~270k BTC in June ran through OTC/offshore channels while the Coinbase gap stayed negative — accumulation is real but deliberately NOT hitting the tape that moves US price in size.
Fear & Greed ~20 (Extreme Fear), briefly ticked to 27 then faded back — the failure to hold the bounce in sentiment mirrors the failure to hold price bounces.
CLARITY Act prediction-market odds collapsed to ~42% from ~73% earlier in the year — a leading legislative gauge the price feeds do not show; XRP headline risk is skewed down into the markup.
Oil +~5% on the Jul-8 Iran ceasefire collapse feeds directly into the Fed's cited inflation risk — a second-order crypto headwind (raises hot-CPI odds for ~Jul 14) that is not on any crypto screen.
SUI's Jul-1 unlock (13.72M / ~$9.4M) is part of a ~$73M weekly unlock wave — persistent programmatic sell pressure that a spot chart alone will not attribute.
Clearstream (Deutsche Börse) quietly added SOL custody Jul 8 — EU institutional rails expanding even as US ETF inflows get absorbed by unlocks; a slow-burn structural positive not in the price yet.
